subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Key Components
- **183 Dragon Cards** — each with unique abilities, costs, and habitats
- **3 Cavern Boards** per player (Crimson Cavern, Golden Grotto, Amethyst Abyss)
- **Player Mats** — track your resources and round goals
- **Dragon Guild Track** — shared board for end-game scoring
- **Resource tokens**: Meat, Gold, Crystals, Milk, Eggs
- **Cave Cards** — bonus cards earned by excavating deeper into caverns
- **Round Goal Tiles** — 4 goals (one per round) for bonus points
- **Objective Cards** — personal end-game scoring goals
- **Excavation markers** — track how deep you've dug into each cavern

subtopics.title: Initial Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Setup Steps
1. Each player takes **3 Cavern Boards** and arranges them left to right: Crimson, Golden, Amethyst
2. Place **excavation markers** at the leftmost position of each cavern
3. Shuffle the **Dragon Card deck** and place face-down; reveal 3 face-up
4. Set up the **Dragon Guild Track** in the center
5. Place **4 Round Goal Tiles** randomly (one per round)
6. Deal each player **5 Dragon Cards** and **2 Objective Cards** — keep 3 dragons and 1 objective
7. Give each player starting resources: **2 Meat, 2 Gold, 2 Crystals**
8. Determine first player randomly

#### Player Area Layout
Your 3 cavern boards represent the different environments dragons inhabit. Each cavern has 5 slots for dragons (played left to right) and a depth track for excavation.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Structure
Wyrmspan plays over **4 rounds**. Each round, players take turns clockwise. On your turn, do **one** of these actions:

**1. Play a Dragon Card**
- Choose a dragon from your hand
- Pay its resource cost (shown on the card)
- Place it in the matching cavern (each dragon specifies which cavern it can go in)
- It must go in the **leftmost empty slot** of that cavern
- Activate the dragon's "when played" ability (if any)
- Then activate all previously played dragons in that cavern from left to right

**2. Excavate a Cavern**
- Choose one of your 3 caverns
- Move your excavation marker one space deeper
- Gain the reward shown on that excavation space
- If the space has a **Cave Card icon**, draw a cave card for a bonus

**3. Gain Coins**
- Take **3 coins** from the supply (coins are wild resources)

#### End of Round
When all players have used their turns for the round, score the **Round Goal** and begin the next round.

subtopics.title: The Three Caverns
subtopics.content:
#### Crimson Cavern (Left)
- **Theme**: Aggressive/combat dragons
- **When you play here**: Gain coins and resources from activated dragon abilities
- Dragons in this cavern often provide immediate resource gains

#### Golden Grotto (Center)
- **Theme**: Resource-generating dragons
- **When you play here**: Produce eggs, gold, and other resources
- This cavern is the engine-building heart of your strategy
- Many dragons here convert resources or generate eggs for points

#### Amethyst Abyss (Right)
- **Theme**: Card-drawing and special ability dragons
- **When you play here**: Draw cards, gain cave cards, trigger special effects
- Dragons here help you find the right cards and set up combos

#### Excavation
Each cavern can be excavated deeper to unlock bonuses. Excavating costs a turn but gives you resources, cave cards, and makes future dragon plays more powerful.

subtopics.title: Scoring & End Game
subtopics.content:
#### Game End
The game ends after **Round 4** is completed.

#### Victory Point Sources
1. **Dragon card VP** — Each dragon in your caverns has a VP value printed on it
2. **Eggs** — Each egg token is worth **1 VP**
3. **Dragon Guild Track** — Points based on how far you've advanced
4. **Round Goals** — VP earned from meeting round objectives
5. **Objective Cards** — Personal end-game scoring based on your dragons
6. **Cave Cards** — Some cave cards provide end-game VP bonuses
7. **Cached resources** — Some dragons let you cache resources on them for VP

#### Winner
The player with the most VP wins. Ties broken by most remaining resources.

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Key Strategic Principles

- **Build an engine early.** Your first 1-2 rounds should focus on playing dragons that generate resources, so later rounds you can play expensive high-VP dragons.
- **Excavate strategically.** Don't just play dragons — excavating gives crucial resources and cave cards. A good rhythm is excavate early, play dragons mid-game.
- **Watch the round goals.** Each round has a specific scoring goal. Timing your plays to score these can be worth 5+ VP per round.
- **Eggs are efficient VP.** Dragons that produce eggs are strong because eggs count as VP AND some dragons need eggs as costs.
- **Don't hoard cards.** Having lots of cards in hand doesn't score points. Play dragons to get their VP and abilities on the board.
- **Balance your caverns.** Each cavern chain activates when you play there. A long chain in Golden Grotto can generate massive resources each time you play.
- **Coins are flexible but weak.** Taking 3 coins is a safe action but slow. Prefer playing dragons or excavating when possible.

#### Common Mistakes
- Playing too many cheap dragons early instead of building resource engines
- Ignoring the Dragon Guild Track (it can be worth 15+ VP)
- Forgetting to check your Objective Card when choosing which dragons to play
